description: HP 8663A synthesized signal generator (100 kHz to 2560 MHz, high performance). Legacy HP-IB (IEEE-488) instrument using two-letter function/units program codes, NOT SCPI. Program codes correspond to front-panel keystrokes; value-selectable parameters use a Function-Data-Units sequence (e.g. FR 250 MZ). Program codes may be upper or lower case; spaces and other non-recognized characters are ignored. The Generator recognizes only the ASCII characters LF ! + - . 0-9 a-z A-Z @. Multiple program codes may be concatenated into one Data string, optionally separated by ; (e.g. FR 1,200,000 HZ;AP -30DM). Two execution modes are selectable: Immediate (@3, power-up default; one End-of-String per character) and Deferred (@2; up to 82 characters executed on an EOS = LF, ! or bus END message). The Generator can talk only to return the RQS mask value, the Status key message, learn-mode strings and the IEEE-488 Status Byte; there is no identify (IDN-style) query.

commands.name: frequency
commands.description: Carrier (RF output) frequency function entry. Follow with a numeric value and a frequency unit code (HZ, KZ, MZ or GZ). Range 100 kHz to 2560 MHz (settable down to 1 kHz, unspecified). Resolution 0.1 Hz below 640 MHz, 0.2 Hz to 1280 MHz, 0.4 Hz above. Also serves as the center frequency for Span sweep. Default after System Clear: 100.0 MHz.

commands.name: amplitude
commands.description: RF output amplitude function entry. Follow with a numeric value (leading sign allowed under HP-IB only, e.g. +13 DM) and an amplitude unit code; otherwise the sign must be carried in the unit code (+D or -D). Range +16 to -129.9 dBm (999 mV to 0.072 uV), 0.1 dB resolution.

parameters.name: amplitude
parameters.range: +16 to -129.9 dBm (DM=dBm w/sign on data, +D=+dBm, -D=-dBm, DB=dB, MV=mV, UV=uV)

commands.name: amplitude_off
commands.description: Turn the RF output amplitude off (net output less than -150 dBm). AMPLITUDE readout displays '---'. Equivalent to selecting AMPLITUDE then FCTN OFF.

commands.name: am_depth
commands.description: Amplitude modulation depth function entry. Follow with a numeric value and the percent unit code (PC). Depth 0 to 95 %, 0.1 % resolution. Select the modulation source with M1/M2/M3/M4 and the rate with MOD FREQ (MF). To turn AM off select AM then FCTN OFF (FO).

commands.name: fm_deviation
commands.description: Frequency modulation peak deviation function entry. Follow with a numeric value and the kHz unit code (KZ is the only valid unit). Maximum peak deviation depends on carrier band (e.g. up to 100 kHz below 120 MHz, up to 400 kHz from 1280 MHz). Select source with M1/M2/M3/M4 and rate with MOD FREQ (MF). Default after System Clear: 20.0 kHz. Turn FM off with AM/FM/PM then FCTN OFF (FO).

parameters.name: deviation
parameters.range: carrier-band dependent (e.g. <=100 kHz below 120 MHz, <=400 kHz above 1280 MHz)

commands.name: phase_modulation_deviation
commands.description: Phase modulation (front-panel label phi-M) peak deviation function entry. Follow with a numeric value and the deg unit code (DG is the only valid unit). Maximum peak deviation depends on carrier band (e.g. 100 deg below 120 MHz, up to 400 deg from 1280 MHz), 1-4 deg resolution. Select source with M1/M2/M3/M4 and rate with MOD FREQ (MF). Default after System Clear: 20 deg. Phase modulation requires Option 002.

commands.name: bpsk
commands.description: Select BPSK (binary phase-shift keying) modulation.

commands.name: pulse_modulation
commands.description: Select pulse modulation (available for RF frequencies below 50 MHz, unspecified).

commands.name: modulation_frequency
commands.description: Internal/external modulation rate (MOD FREQ) function entry. Follow with a numeric value and a unit code (HZ or KZ). Variable rate 10 Hz to 99.9 kHz; resolution varies with rate. Default after System Clear: 1 kHz.

commands.name: modulation_off
commands.description: Turn off all modulation circuitry (equivalent to Blue Key, MOD OFF).

commands.name: mod_source_int_400
commands.description: Select internal 400 Hz fixed modulation source (INT 400).

commands.name: mod_source_int_1k
commands.description: Select internal 1 kHz fixed modulation source (INT 1k).

commands.name: mod_source_ext_ac
commands.description: Select external ac-coupled modulation source (EXT AC).

commands.name: mod_source_ext_dc
commands.description: Select external dc-coupled modulation source (EXT DC).

commands.name: fctn_off
commands.description: Turn off the last-selected front-panel function (FCTN OFF). For modulation, selecting the modulation type (AM/FM/PM) then FO turns that modulation off.

commands.name: start_frequency
commands.description: RF sweep Start frequency function entry (START FREQ). Follow with a numeric value and a unit code. Sweep width 100 kHz to 2560 MHz.

commands.name: stop_frequency
commands.description: RF sweep Stop frequency function entry (STOP FREQ). Follow with a numeric value and a unit code. Sweep width 100 kHz to 2560 MHz.

commands.name: span_frequency
commands.description: RF sweep Span frequency function entry (SPAN FREQ); total peak-to-peak deviation about the center frequency set with FR. Follow with a numeric value and a unit code. Span width 0.2 Hz to 2560 MHz (depends on center frequency).

commands.name: set_step_size
commands.description: Set a constant sweep step size of your choice (SET SIZE). Follow with a numeric value and a unit code. Resolution 0.1 Hz below 640 MHz, 0.2 Hz to 1280 MHz, 0.4 Hz above.

commands.name: steps_lin_100
commands.description: Set linear sweep to 100 steps (step size = sweepwidth/100).

commands.name: steps_lin_1000
commands.description: Set linear sweep to 1000 steps (step size = sweepwidth/1000).

commands.name: steps_log_10pct
commands.description: Set logarithmic sweep with 10% steps (initial step = 10% of start frequency; subsequent steps increase 10%).

commands.name: steps_log_1pct
commands.description: Set logarithmic sweep with 1% steps (initial step = 1% of start frequency; subsequent steps increase 1%).

commands.name: sweep_off
commands.description: Turn sweep off (MODE OFF).

commands.name: sweep_auto
commands.description: Select Auto (continuous) sweep mode.

commands.name: sweep_manual
commands.description: Select Manual sweep mode (controlled by the knob).

commands.name: sweep_single
commands.description: Select Single sweep mode.

commands.name: sweep_time_per_step_05ms
commands.description: Set sweep time per step to 0.5 ms (nominal).

commands.name: sweep_time_per_step_1ms
commands.description: Set sweep time per step to 1 ms.

commands.name: sweep_time_per_step_2ms
commands.description: Set sweep time per step to 2 ms.

commands.name: sweep_time_per_step_10ms
commands.description: Set sweep time per step to 10 ms.

commands.name: sweep_time_per_step_100ms
commands.description: Set sweep time per step to 100 ms.

commands.name: marker_1
commands.description: Marker 1. As a frequency entry follow with a value and unit; as an increment/copy target use the X1 code alone.

commands.name: marker_2
commands.description: Marker 2 (frequency entry or selection).

commands.name: marker_3
commands.description: Marker 3 (frequency entry or selection).

commands.name: marker_4
commands.description: Marker 4 (frequency entry or selection).

commands.name: marker_5
commands.description: Marker 5 (frequency entry or selection).

commands.name: marker_off
commands.description: Turn the selected marker off (MARKER OFF).

commands.name: markers_all_off
commands.description: Turn all markers off (ALL OFF). Equivalent to Blue Key then MARKER OFF (BL X6).

commands.name: rss_off
commands.description: Disable HP-IB Remote Stepped Sweep and reset the frequency readout (Remote Stepped Sweep Off).

commands.name: rss_with_display
commands.description: Enable Remote Stepped Sweep with display update. Controller sends one program code per step.

commands.name: rss_without_display
commands.description: Enable Remote Stepped Sweep without display update (frequency stepped, readout not updated).

commands.name: rss_execute
commands.description: Execute one step of the Remote Stepped Sweep (RSS Execute). Sent once per sweep step after Y1 or Y2.

commands.name: increment_up
commands.description: Increase the last-selected parameter value by its increment (INCREMENT up arrow). Precede with the parameter's function code to choose which parameter is stepped.

commands.name: increment_down
commands.description: Decrease the last-selected parameter value by its increment (INCREMENT down arrow). Precede with the parameter's function code.

commands.name: increment_set
commands.description: Set the increment step size of a parameter (INCR SET): select a function code, then IS, then Data and Units. Subsequent UP/DN steps use this size.

commands.name: resolution_x10
commands.description: Enable the knob / set knob resolution times 10 (Resolution X10, ON).

commands.name: resolution_div10
commands.description: Enable the knob / set knob resolution divided by 10 (Resolution 1/10, ON).

commands.name: knob_off
commands.description: Turn the knob off (Knob Off).

commands.name: knob_hold
commands.description: Hold knob control on the current function while keyboard control shifts to another (Knob Hold).

commands.name: knob_increment
commands.description: Select the knob increment mode (Knob Increment).

commands.name: knob_cw
commands.description: Knob clockwise / up one step (Knob CW). Must be sent ten times to execute one step, except in Manual Sweep mode or when Special 94/95 is enabled (then one code = one step). Does not require the knob to be enabled first.

commands.name: knob_ccw
commands.description: Knob counter-clockwise / down one step (Knob CCW). Must be sent ten times to execute one step, except in Manual Sweep mode or when Special 94/95 is enabled (then one code = one step).

commands.name: store
commands.description: Store the full front-panel configuration into a storage register (1-9). Press STORE then a register number.

commands.name: recall
commands.description: Recall a front-panel configuration from a storage register (1-9). Press RECALL then a register number.

commands.name: sequence
commands.description: Recall register contents one register per SEQ keystroke, in the predetermined recall order set with SS.

commands.name: set_sequence
commands.description: Set the register recall order (SET SEQ): SS followed by the register numbers in the desired recall order, then ST (STORE). Maximum ten digits; register numbers may repeat. Equivalent to BL ST.

commands.name: auto_sequence
commands.description: Initiate continuous (auto) recall of registers in the predetermined sequence (AUTO SEQ). Equivalent to BL SQ. Stop by selecting any key except STATUS.

commands.name: blue_key
commands.description: Blue (shift) key. Precedes a second key code to invoke the blue-labeled function (e.g. BL ST = SET SEQ, BL SQ = AUTO SEQ, BL X6 = ALL OFF, BL AP = SPECIAL). Also returns the Generator to local mode (if local lockout is not in effect).

commands.name: backspace
commands.description: Backspace (delete last entered digit during data entry).

commands.name: special_function
commands.description: Invoke a Special Function: SP followed by the special-function number. Equivalent to Blue Key then SPECIAL (BL AP). Documented special functions include: 00 System Clear; 2.0/2.1 power-up system-clear disable/enable; 10/11/12 Frequency Offset off/positive/negative; 20/21 ALC bandwidth normal/reduced; 30/31 Amplitude Reference off/on; 30/32 Microvolt Reference off/on; 41 Modulation Sweep; 70/71 external phiM input impedance; 81 Amplitude conversion V->dBm; 82 display HP-IB address; 85/86 Amplitude Correction off/on; 87 Amplitude Correction in sweep mode; 88 Auto-Sequence indicator; 91 Frequency Hopping; 92/94/95 Knob increments/revolution; 93 Manual Level-Control.

commands.name: configure_trigger
commands.description: Configure Trigger: CT followed by the program code of the operation to be performed on a subsequent trigger. The trigger is then fired with TR (or bus GET).

commands.name: trigger
commands.description: Trigger: execute the operation previously assigned with Configure Trigger (CT). Responds equally to the bus GET command.

commands.name: write_rqs_mask
commands.description: Write (set) the Request Service (RQS) mask: @1 followed by a data message giving the mask value. Controls which conditions assert SRQ.

commands.name: read_rqs_mask
commands.description: Read the Request Service (RQS) mask value. Send RM, then address the Generator to talk to receive the mask value.

commands.name: read_status_message
commands.description: Read the STATUS key message. Send MS, then address the Generator to talk to receive the status information.

commands.name: front_panel_learn
commands.description: Initiate Front-Panel Learn mode: send L1, then address the Generator to talk; it returns the front-panel configuration learn string. Re-sending the string later restores that configuration.

commands.name: fast_learn
commands.description: Initiate 'Fast' Learn mode: send L2, then address the Generator to talk; it returns the complete instrument configuration learn string. Re-sending the string later restores that configuration.

commands.name: deferred_execution_mode
commands.description: Select Deferred execution mode: the Generator buffers up to 82 characters and executes the string on receipt of an EOS message (LF, ! or bus END).

commands.name: immediate_execution_mode
commands.description: Select Immediate execution mode (power-up default): the Generator processes each character as it is received.
